title: MiroThinker-1.7 & H1: Towards Heavy-Duty Research Agents via Verification
task: Complex long-horizon reasoning for research tasks
model: (transformer) MiroThinker-1.7 & MiroThinker-H1
inputs: user queries and tool APIs for open-web research, scientific reasoning, and financial analysis
outputs: structured reasoning plans with verified intermediate steps and final answers
key_results: state-of-the-art performance on deep research tasks with local and global-level verification; open-source 1.7-mini variant available
comments: Integration of verification into reasoning is commendable, though the specific performance metrics relative to prior work are not quantified in the abstract.

title: InCoder-32B: Code Foundation Model for Industrial Scenarios
task: Code generation for industrial domains
model: (transformer) InCoder-32B
inputs: natural language descriptions of programming tasks in specialized domains
outputs: code solutions for chip design, GPU kernels, embedded systems, compiler optimization, and 3D modeling
key_results: competitive performance on 14 general code benchmarks and strong baselines on 9 industrial benchmarks; extends context from 8K to 128K tokens
comments: Notable for combining general pretraining with domain-specific annealing and execution-grounded verification, addressing a real gap in industrial code generation.

title: Qianfan-OCR: A Unified End-to-End Model for Document Intelligence
task: Document parsing and layout analysis via image-to-Markdown
model: (vision-language) Qianfan-OCR 4B
inputs: Document images with optional prompts
outputs: Markdown text with layout structure, tables, charts, answers
key_results: 93.12 on OmniDocBench v1.5; first among end-to-end models; surpasses Gemini-3.1-Pro on KIE
comments: Layout-as-Thought mechanism recovers explicit layout reasoning which is typically lost in end-to-end approaches.

title: Kinema4D: Kinematic 4D World Modeling for Spatiotemporal Embodied Simulation
task: Action-conditioned 4D robot simulation from video generation
model: (generative) Kinema4D with URDF kinematics
inputs: Robot control trajectories and environmental context
outputs: Synchronized RGB and pointmap sequences of robot-world interactions
key_results: Demonstrates zero-shot transfer capability with physically-plausible geometry-consistent interactions
comments: First work to model robot-world interactions as true 4D spatiotemporal events rather than 2D or static-guided approaches.

title: Demystifing Video Reasoning
task: Understanding reasoning mechanisms in video generation models
model: (diffusion transformer) video diffusion models
inputs: prompts or conditioning signals for video generation tasks requiring reasoning
outputs: generated video frames with reasoning across diffusion denoising steps
key_results: identified Chain-of-Steps (CoS) as primary reasoning mechanism instead of Chain-of-Frames; ensemble latent trajectories improve performance
comments: Provides valuable mechanistic insights into how diffusion models reason, but the practical improvements from proposed training-free strategies are not quantified.

title: WorldCam: Interactive Autoregressive 3D Gaming Worlds with Camera Pose as a Unifying Geometric Representation
task: Interactive 3D gaming world generation with precise action control
model: (diffusion transformer) video diffusion transformer
inputs: user actions represented as 6-DoF camera poses in Lie algebra; historical observations indexed by global camera pose
outputs: video frames of interactive gaming environment with 3D spatial consistency over long horizons
key_results: substantially outperforms state-of-the-art in action controllability and 3D spatial consistency; introduces 3,000-minute authentic gameplay dataset with camera trajectories
comments: Clever use of camera pose as unifying geometric representation is elegant, though quantitative comparisons on standard metrics are absent from abstract.

title: TRUST-SQL: Tool-Integrated Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-SQL over Unknown Schemas
task: Text-to-SQL parsing with unknown database schemas
model: (RL with GRPO) agent-based system with LLM backbone
inputs: natural language queries; access to unknown schemas via tool calls
outputs: executable SQL queries with metadata verification
key_results: 30.6% absolute improvement for 4B variant, 16.6% for 8B; matches schema-prefilled baselines without pre-loaded metadata
comments: Dual-Track GRPO with token-level masked advantages is novel; 9.9% relative improvement over standard GRPO demonstrates effective credit assignment.

title: Thinking in Uncertainty: Mitigating Hallucinations in MLRMs with Latent Entropy-Aware Decoding
task: Mitigating hallucinations in visual question answering by MLRMs
model: (transformer) multimodal large reasoning models
inputs: images and questions; token probability distributions during high-entropy reasoning stages
outputs: answers with reduced hallucinations via entropy-aware decoding
key_results: LEAD strategy effectively mitigates hallucinations across multiple MLLMs and benchmarks; uses probability-weighted embeddings under high entropy
comments: Interesting approach leveraging entropy patterns in transition words, but specific quantitative improvements are not provided in abstract.

title: Online Experiential Learning for Language Models
task: Continuous online improvement of language models from deployment experience
model: (transformer) language models at multiple scales
inputs: interaction trajectories from real-world deployment; user-side experience
outputs: improved model parameters via on-policy context distillation
key_results: consistent improvements over iterations; enhanced task accuracy and token efficiency while preserving OOD performance on text-based games
comments: Clean separation of knowledge extraction and parameter consolidation is appealing, but experiments are limited to game environments rather than broader deployment scenarios.

title: FinToolBench: Evaluating LLM Agents for Real-World Financial Tool Use
task: Evaluating LLM agents for real-world financial tool use
model: (LLM agents) various foundation models with financial tool APIs
inputs: financial queries requiring tool execution; 760 executable financial tools
outputs: evaluation scores on timeliness, intent type, and regulatory alignment; FATR baseline results
key_results: FinToolBench comprises 760 executable tools with 295 tool-required queries; introduces finance-critical evaluation dimensions beyond binary success
comments: Valuable contribution addressing real-world finance domain, though this is primarily a benchmark paper without strong baseline results reported.

title: WiT: Waypoint Diffusion Transformers via Trajectory Conflict Navigation
task: High-quality pixel-space image generation via trajectory disentanglement
model: (transformer) Waypoint Diffusion Transformers (WiT)
inputs: Noisy images and semantic waypoint guidance from vision models
outputs: High-fidelity RGB images
key_results: 2.2x faster JiT training convergence than pixel-space baselines on ImageNet 256×256
comments: Elegantly solves optimal transport path conflicts in pixel space by introducing semantic waypoints as intermediate checkpoints.

title: Rethinking UMM Visual Generation: Masked Modeling for Efficient Image-Only Pre-training
task: Efficient visual generation pre-training for unified multimodal models
model: (transformer) IOMM-B 3.6B
inputs: Unlabeled images (stage 1), then mixed unlabeled and paired data (stage 2)
outputs: Generated images with instruction alignment
key_results: 0.89 GenEval and 0.55 WISE; trained in ~1050 GPU hours; surpasses BAGEL-7B and BLIP3-o-4B
comments: Removes paired-data dependency in expensive pre-training by leveraging abundant unlabeled images via masked modeling.

title: GradMem: Learning to Write Context into Memory with Test-Time Gradient Descent
task: Compressed memory for long-context language models via test-time optimization
model: (transformer-based) GradMem with gradient descent
inputs: Long context documents and queries without context access
outputs: Answers retrieved from compact prefix memory tokens
key_results: Outperforms forward-only memory writers; scales memory capacity more effectively with additional gradient steps
comments: Loss-driven write operation via gradient descent is novel—most memory methods rely purely on forward passes.

title: SegviGen: Repurposing 3D Generative Model for Part Segmentation
task: 3D part segmentation via repurposing 3D generative models
model: (3D generative model) pretrained native 3D generator with segmentation adapter
inputs: 3D geometry; optional interactive or 2D guidance
outputs: part segmentation masks via distinctive part colorization
key_results: 40% improvement on interactive part segmentation, 15% on full segmentation; uses only 0.32% of labeled training data versus prior work
comments: Clever repurposing of pretrained priors for a discriminative task; significant data efficiency gain validates transferability of 3D generative models.

title: AgentProcessBench: Diagnosing Step-Level Process Quality in Tool-Using Agents
task: Evaluating step-level effectiveness in tool-using agent trajectories
model: (LLM agents) various policy models
inputs: tool-augmented trajectories with 8,509 human-labeled step annotations
outputs: per-step correctness labels and agent quality metrics
key_results: identified key challenges: weaker models show inflated step correctness due to early termination; process signals complement outcome supervision for test-time scaling
comments: First benchmark dedicated to step-level evaluation in realistic tool use; ternary labeling scheme and error propagation rules improve annotation reliability.

title: SocialOmni: Benchmarking Audio-Visual Social Interactivity in Omni Models
task: Evaluating audio-visual social interactivity in omni-modal models
model: (multimodal) omni-modal large language models
inputs: audio-visual dialogue with speaker cues and interruption opportunities
outputs: speaker identification, interruption timing, and natural interruption generation
key_results: decoupling between perceptual accuracy and interaction generation quality across 12 OLMs; significant variance in social-interaction capabilities
comments: Operationalizes underexplored dimension of conversational AI; reveals important gap between understanding and appropriate social response generation.

title: SWE-Skills-Bench: Do Agent Skills Actually Help in Real-World Software Engineering?
task: Evaluating marginal utility of agent skills in software engineering
model: (LLM agents) with injected procedural skills
inputs: GitHub repositories, requirements, and optional skills
outputs: Pass/fail task completion with execution-based verification
key_results: 39 of 49 skills yield zero improvement; average gain only +1.2%; only 7 skills produce >+5% gains
comments: Critical finding: skills are narrower interventions than assumed; utility strongly depends on domain fit and contextual compatibility.

title: Efficient Reasoning on the Edge
task: Efficient on-device reasoning for small language models via adapter tuning and budget forcing
model: (adapter-based) LoRA adapters on small LLMs (Qwen2.5-7B) with dynamic switching
inputs: prompts requiring reasoning; budget constraints for token generation and memory
outputs: concise reasoning traces and final answers optimized for edge deployment
key_results: practical reasoning on mobile with strict resource constraints; dynamic adapter switching and KV-cache sharing reduce overhead; 20x inference speedup potential
comments: Practical contribution addressing real constraint of on-device deployment; budget forcing via RL to compress reasoning while maintaining accuracy is pragmatic.

title: One-Eval: An Agentic System for Automated and Traceable LLM Evaluation
task: Automating LLM evaluation from natural-language requests
model: (agentic system) LLM-based evaluation orchestrator
inputs: natural-language evaluation requests; optional human edits and rollback
outputs: executable evaluation workflows with task-aware metrics and decision-oriented reporting
key_results: end-to-end evaluation execution from diverse NL requests with minimal user effort; human-in-the-loop checkpoints preserve auditability
comments: Addresses real pain point of heterogeneous evaluation codebases; focus on reproducibility and human oversight is commendable for industrial settings.

title: M^3: Dense Matching Meets Multi-View Foundation Models for Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM
task: Monocular video SLAM with dense correspondence matching
model: (vision foundation model + Gaussian splatting) M³ system combining multi-view foundation model matching with SLAM
inputs: uncalibrated monocular video stream
outputs: camera poses and 3D scene reconstruction via Gaussian splatting
key_results: 64.3% ATE RMSE reduction vs VGGT-SLAM 2.0; 2.11 dB PSNR improvement over ARTDECO on ScanNet++
comments: Augments multi-view foundation models with dedicated matching head for fine-grained correspondences; addresses critical bottleneck in geometric optimization.

title: Reliable Reasoning in SVG-LLMs via Multi-Task Multi-Reward Reinforcement Learning
task: SVG generation with structured reasoning and code refinement
model: (vision-language model with chain-of-thought) CTRL-S using GRPO and multi-reward optimization
inputs: text descriptions or images
outputs: SVG code with group-level structure and visual SVG rendering
key_results: higher task success rates, superior code quality, and exceptional visual fidelity; multi-reward framework incorporates DINO, image-text similarity, format, and efficiency rewards
comments: Introduction of chain-of-thought reasoning for SVG generation is novel; multi-reward optimization with GRPO demonstrates effective handling of competing objectives.

title: Semi-Autonomous Formalization of the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau Equilibrium
task: Formal verification of plasma physics equilibrium in Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau system
model: (AI reasoning + symbolic proof) Gemini DeepThink + Claude + Aristotle prover + Lean 4 kernel
inputs: mathematical conjectures about plasma equilibrium
outputs: fully formal Lean 4 proofs verified by kernel
key_results: complete formalization of VML equilibrium characterization; 229 human prompts, 213 commits, $200 cost, 10 days with one mathematician
comments: Remarkable demonstration of AI-assisted formal verification; detailed documentation of failure modes (hypothesis creep, definition bugs, agent avoidance) provides valuable lessons for future work.

title: From Passive Observer to Active Critic: Reinforcement Learning Elicits Process Reasoning for Robotic Manipulation
task: Process monitoring and failure detection for long-horizon robotic manipulation
model: (video MLLM with RL) PRIMO R1 (7B), transforms passive observer to active critic
inputs: video sequences anchored between initial and current state; optional task goal
outputs: progress estimation and failure detection via chain-of-thought reasoning
key_results: 50% reduction in MAE vs specialized baselines; 67.0% accuracy on RoboFail, surpassing OpenAI o1 by 6.0%; strong zero-shot generalization
comments: Clever use of structured temporal input anchoring; outcome-based RL for eliciting process reasoning is an effective way to shift from passive recognition to active evaluation.

title: SK-Adapter: Skeleton-Based Structural Control for Native 3D Generation
task: Skeletal control for native 3D object generation
model: (3D diffusion model) frozen generative backbone with lightweight SK-Adapter
inputs: 3D skeleton (joint coordinates and topology) as structural control signal
outputs: high-quality 3D meshes with precise skeletal articulation and preserved texture
key_results: robust structural control while preserving geometry and texture quality; enables local 3D editing via skeletal guidance; Objaverse-TMS dataset released with 24k samples
comments: Treating 3D skeleton as first-class control signal is intuitive; lightweight adapter design preserves generative priors while enabling precise manipulation.

title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
task: Long-context information extraction via self-reflective program search
model: (recursive decomposition) SRLM augmenting Recursive Language Models with uncertainty-aware self-reflection
inputs: long-context documents; prompts requiring reasoning over extended context
outputs: answers derived from recursively decomposed context interactions
key_results: up to 22% improvement over RLM under same time budget; self-reflection (consistency, reasoning length, confidence) outperforms recursion alone across diverse benchmarks
comments: Important finding that self-reflection is more critical than recursion itself; demonstrates that heuristic signals can guide effective long-context handling.

title: Omnilingual MT: Machine Translation for 1,600 Languages
task: Machine translation for 1,600 languages with massive multilingual coverage
model: (decoder-only and encoder-decoder) OMT-LLaMA and OMT-NLLB (1B–8B)
inputs: Source language text in 1,600 language pairs
outputs: Target language translations
key_results: 1B–8B models match or exceed 70B LLM baseline; enables coherent generation for previously unsupported languages
comments: Comprehensive data strategy and specialization show clear advantage over generic LLMs; first system at 1,600-language scale.

title: MolmoB0T: Large-Scale Simulation Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation
task: Zero-shot sim-to-real transfer for robot manipulation
model: (vision-language policy) MolmoBot based on Molmo2 with flow-matching action head; plus Pi0-replica and SPOC variants
inputs: visual observations from robot cameras; task descriptions
outputs: continuous action commands for articulated manipulation and mobile manipulation
key_results: 79.2% success on tabletop pick-and-place in real world; outperforms π₀.₅ at 39.2%; zero-shot transfer to unseen objects without real-world fine-tuning; 1.8M synthetic trajectories
comments: Strong result demonstrating that procedural sim-to-real gap can be bridged with sufficient scale and diversity; release of 1.8M trajectories and open-source pipeline valuable for community.

title: Mixture of Style Experts for Diverse Image Stylization
task: Semantic-aware diverse image stylization via mixture of experts
model: (diffusion + MoE) StyleExpert
inputs: Content image and style embedding from unified style encoder
outputs: Stylized images preserving semantics and material details
key_results: Outperforms existing approaches; generalizes to unseen styles with improved semantic and material preservation
comments: MoE routing allows handling diverse styles across multiple semantic levels—shallow textures to deep semantics.

title: ViT-AdaLA: Adapting Vision Transformers with Linear Attention
task: Efficient adaptation of Vision Transformers with linear attention
model: (Vision Transformer) ViT-AdaLA
inputs: Vision foundation models and downstream vision tasks
outputs: Linearized ViT parameters for classification and segmentation
key_results: Effective knowledge transfer from softmax ViTs to linear attention variants; demonstrated on classification and segmentation tasks
comments: Three-stage alignment strategy (attention, feature, fine-tuning) addresses layer-wise error accumulation, but specific performance gains versus baselines are not reported in abstract.

title: FlashSampling: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Sampling
task: Fast and memory-efficient categorical sampling for LLM decoding
model: (GPU kernel) fused sampling primitive integrating into LM-head matmul
inputs: logits tile-by-tile on-chip; Gumbel noise injection
outputs: exact categorical samples without materializing full logits tensor in HBM
key_results: up to 19% reduction in time per output token in vLLM; speeds up kernel-level decode across H100, H200, B200, B300 GPUs
comments: Elegant kernel-level optimization exploiting argmax properties; demonstrates that exact sampling can be efficiently fused into matmul without approximation.

title: Sparking Scientific Creativity via LLM-Driven Interdisciplinary Inspiration
task: Identifying interdisciplinary insights for creative scientific reasoning
model: (LLM-based) Idea-Catalyst framework
inputs: Abstract research goal
outputs: Ranked source domains with recontextualized interdisciplinary insights
key_results: +21% improvement in novelty; +16% in insightfulness while remaining grounded in original research
comments: Metacognitive features (goal assessment, challenge identification, impact ranking) drive creative reasoning without premature solution anchoring.

title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents
task: Multi-step task planning for multimodal agents
model: (reinforcement learning) TraceR1
inputs: Computer-use and tool-use task instructions
outputs: Action sequences with anticipated future states
key_results: Substantial improvements in planning stability and execution robustness across seven benchmarks; improved generalization over reactive baselines
comments: Two-stage RL approach (trajectory-level + grounded fine-tuning) is well-motivated, though absolute performance metrics are not quantified in the abstract.

title: Learning Human-Object Interaction for 3D Human Pose Estimation from LiDAR Point Clouds
task: 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR under human-object interactions
model: (point cloud network) HOIL framework with contrastive learning and attention pooling
inputs: LiDAR point clouds of humans interacting with objects
outputs: 3D keypoint coordinates for human pose; temporal refinement via contact cues
key_results: HOIL effectively resolves spatial ambiguity and class imbalance in interaction regions; robust pose estimation in cluttered backgrounds; contact-aware temporal refinement improves per-frame predictions
comments: Well-motivated approach addressing two concrete challenges (spatial ambiguity, class imbalance) in autonomous driving context; temporal refinement is practical addition.

title: Polyglot-Lion: Efficient Multilingual ASR for Singapore via Balanced Fine-Tuning of Qwen3-ASR
task: Multilingual automatic speech recognition for Singapore languages
model: (fine-tuned ASR) Qwen3-ASR-0.6B and 1.7B with balanced language sampling
inputs: speech audio in English, Mandarin, Tamil, Malay
outputs: transcribed text with implicit language identification from audio
key_results: Polyglot-Lion-1.7B achieves 14.85 average error rate competitive with 6x larger MERaLiON-2-10B (14.32); 20x faster inference; $81 training cost vs $18,862
comments: Impressive efficiency gains through balanced fine-tuning strategy; elimination of language tags forces implicit learning, improving cross-lingual generalization.

title: OneWorld: Taming Scene Generation with 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder
task: 3D scene generation with cross-view consistency via unified 3D representation
model: (3D diffusion) OneWorld using 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE) with pretrained 3D foundation models
inputs: scene descriptions or conditions; token-level cross-view correspondence loss
outputs: high-quality 3D scenes with superior cross-view appearance and geometric consistency
key_results: superior cross-view consistency vs 2D-based methods; token-level CVC consistency loss enforces structural alignment; Manifold-Drift Forcing mitigates train-inference bias
comments: Operating diffusion directly in unified 3D space addresses fundamental limitation of 2D latent approaches; augmentation of geometry-centric models with appearance and semantics is principled.

title: CCTU: A Benchmark for Tool Use under Complex Constraints
task: Evaluating LLM tool use under complex constraints
model: (LLM agents) nine state-of-the-art models in thinking and non-thinking modes
inputs: tool-use scenarios with average 7 constraint types; prompts exceeding 4,500 tokens; 12 constraint categories across 4 dimensions
outputs: execution traces with step-level constraint violation validation
key_results: no model achieves >20% completion rate under strict constraint adherence; models violate constraints in >50% of cases; limited self-refinement capability even with feedback
comments: Sobering benchmark revealing critical gap in constraint compliance; dimensional taxonomy (resource, behavior, toolset, response) provides useful structure for future improvements.

title: SuperLocalMemory V3: Information-Geometric Foundations for Zero-LLM Enterprise Agent Memory
task: Persistent agent memory with information-geometric foundations
model: (zero-LLM) SuperLocalMemory V3 with Riemannian geometry
inputs: Conversation history and memory queries
outputs: Retrieved memory contexts with contradiction detection
key_results: +12.7 pp over engineering baselines on LoCoMo; 75% accuracy without cloud; 87.7% with cloud augmentation
comments: Rigorous mathematical foundations (Fisher information, Langevin dynamics, sheaf theory) replacing heuristics; EU AI Act compliant.

title: Theoretical Foundations of Latent Posterior Factors: Formal Guarantees for Multi-Evidence Reasoning
task: Theoretical analysis and formal guarantees for multi-evidence probabilistic reasoning
model: (SPN + VAE) Latent Posterior Factors (LPF)
inputs: Multiple heterogeneous evidence items
outputs: Calibrated probabilistic predictions with uncertainty quantification
key_results: Calibration ECE ≤ ε + C/√K_eff; PAC-Bayes bound gap 0.0085 at N=4200; within 1.12× information-theoretic lower bound
comments: Seven formal guarantees establish trustworthiness; validates on safety-critical applications (diagnosis, compliance, finance).

title: I Know What I Don't Know: Latent Posterior Factor Models for Multi-Evidence Probabilistic Reasoning
task: Multi-evidence probabilistic reasoning with latent uncertainty aggregation
model: (VAE + SPN) LPF-SPN and LPF-Learned
inputs: Multiple unstructured evidence sources
outputs: Calibrated probabilistic predictions with explicit uncertainty
key_results: 97.8% accuracy; ECE 1.4%; substantially outperforms EDL, LLMs, and graph baselines on 8 domains
comments: Bridges latent VAE posteriors with structured SPN inference; enables controlled comparison of probabilistic vs. learned reasoning.

title: HistoAtlas: A Pan-Cancer Morphology Atlas Linking Histomics to Molecular Programs and Clinical Outcomes
task: Pan-cancer morphology-to-outcome linking via histomic analysis
model: (computer vision) HistoAtlas on 6,745 H&E slides
inputs: Diagnostic H&E histopathology images
outputs: 38 interpretable histomic features linked to survival, mutations, immunity, expression
key_results: Covariate-adjusted, multiple-testing corrected; recovers known biology; enables biomarker discovery without specialized staining
comments: Spatially traceable features and compartment-specific immune signals provide interpretability rarely achieved at scale.

title: VAREX: A Benchmark for Multi-Modal Structured Extraction from Documents
task: Multimodal structured data extraction from government forms
model: (multimodal foundation models) evaluated on VAREX benchmark
inputs: Document text, layout-preserving text, image, or text+image
outputs: Structured extracted fields conforming to document schema
key_results: Schema compliance is dominant bottleneck <4B; +81 pp gain with fine-tuning at 2B; layout-text outperforms pixels by +3–18 pp
comments: First benchmark with four controlled input modalities per document; reveals schema echo as critical failure mode below 4B.

title: Chain-of-Trajectories: Unlocking the Intrinsic Generative Optimality of Diffusion Models via Graph-Theoretic Planning
task: Graph-theoretic planning for adaptive diffusion sampling trajectories
model: (diffusion) Chain-of-Trajectories (CoTj) framework
inputs: Noisy images and Diffusion DNA (per-stage denoising difficulty signature)
outputs: Adaptively scheduled high-quality generated images
key_results: Discovers context-aware trajectories; improves output quality and stability; reduces redundant computation
comments: Train-free framework reformulates sampling as DAG planning; enables System 2 deliberative planning versus reflexive System 1.

title: Measuring Primitive Accumulation: An Information-Theoretic Approach to Capitalist Enclosure in PIK2, Indonesia
task: Quantifying capitalist land enclosure via information-geometric and statistical-mechanical analysis
model: (information geometry + Markov chains) Fisher-Rao geodesics on Marxian probability simplex; percolation and fractal analysis
inputs: Sentinel-2 LULC satellite data (2017-2024) at 10m resolution; land-use fractions (Commons, Agrarian, Capital)
outputs: quantitative characterization of spatial transformation velocity, topology, irreversibility; percolation thresholds and fractal dimensions
key_results: transformation pulse of 0.405 rad/yr (2019-2020); absorption times of 46.0 yrs (cropland) and 38.1 yrs (tree cover); built-area self-retention 96.4%; urban boundary fractal dimension 1.316→1.397
comments: Novel application of mathematical physics tools to political economy; information-geometric approach to quantifying enclosure is creative but requires deep domain expertise to validate causal claims.

title: Test-Time Strategies for More Efficient and Accurate Agentic RAG
task: Improving efficiency and accuracy of iterative RAG systems
model: (LLM agent) Search-R1 with modifications
inputs: Complex multi-hop questions
outputs: Retrieved documents and generated answers with reasoning
key_results: 5.6% increase in EM score and 10.5% reduction in retrieval turns compared to Search-R1 baseline on HotpotQA and Natural Questions
comments: Test-time modifications are practical and yield concrete improvements, but the approach is incremental optimization rather than fundamental architectural innovation.

title: RPA directly stimulates Mer3 helicase processivity to ensure normal crossover formation in meiosis
task: Determine how RPA stimulates Mer3 helicase processivity in meiotic crossover formation
model: (experiment) single-molecule magnetic tweezers; (computational) AlphaFold2 structural modeling
inputs: Mer3 helicase, RPA protein, double-stranded DNA, varying DNA tension conditions
outputs: Helicase processivity, crossover frequency, recombination intermediate accumulation, protein localization at DSB sites
key_results: RPA binding directly increases Mer3 processivity under low DNA tension; mer3 RPA-binding mutant shows reduced crossover frequencies and weakened DSB site recruitment
comments: This work elegantly connects molecular interaction specificity to whole-organism meiotic phenotypes via single-molecule mechanics, though the biological relevance of low-tension conditions requires further validation.
hypotheses: RPA directly interacts with Mer3 helicase
RPA interaction enhances Mer3 processivity under low DNA tension
RPA binding is required for normal crossover formation and meiotic fidelity
results: RPA-Mer3 interaction is conserved and specific (cross-linking MS, AlphaFold2)
RPA enhances Mer3 processivity in magnetic tweezers assays at low tension
RPA-binding deficient mer3 mutant exhibits reduced crossovers and DSB recruitment

title: Proton channels govern vesicular carbonate chemistry in mineralizing cells of a marine calcifier
task: Elucidate proton channel control of pH and carbonate chemistry in calcifying vesicles
model: (experiment) sea urchin embryo ACC biomineralization system; electrophysiology; confocal live-cell imaging
inputs: Calcifying cells, vesicles with Ca2+ and carbonate precursors, varying pH and ion conditions
outputs: Intra-vesicular pH, Ca2+ concentration, proton conductance, Otop2l channel activation state, mineral formation rate
key_results: Otop2l proton channel acts as master regulator of membrane proton conductance; Otop2l activated by alkaline pH and Mg2+/Ca2+ ions; high proton permeability generates alkaline vesicular conditions for ACC formation
comments: This work provides valuable mechanistic insight into biomineralization pH control, but the relationship between Otop2l-mediated proton flux and the observed alkaline pH gradient requires clarification of the electrochemical driving forces.
hypotheses: Proton channels regulate intra-vesicular pH in calcifying cells
Otop2l is the primary proton channel controlling carbonate chemistry during ACC formation
Otop2l activation by alkaline pH and divalent cations maintains the pH gradient required for biomineralization
results: Calcifying vesicles show high proton permeability and alkaline pH conditions
Otop2l identified as master regulator of membrane proton conductance in calcifying cells
Otop2l activated by alkaline conditions and Mg2+/Ca2+ ions; alkaline vesicular pH supports mineral precursor formation

title: Mushroom biotech startups help address global challenges
task: review translational pathways from mycology research to commercial products
model: N/A
inputs: mushroom biotechnology innovations and startup ecosystems
outputs: classification of cross-sectoral bioinspired innovation models
key_results: mushrooms identified as platform for sustainable industry and human wellbeing; startups bridge scientific advances to commercial application
comments: Perspective article lacking quantitative validation or mechanistic detail; primarily a conceptual framework for biotech translation.

title: Celcomen: spatial causal disentanglement for single-cell and tissue perturbation modeling
task: disentangle intra- and inter-cellular gene regulation from spatial transcriptomics
model: generative graph neural network with causal inference framework
inputs: spatial transcriptomics data from tissues
outputs: causal gene regulation networks; post-perturbation counterfactual transcriptomics predictions
key_results: validated disentanglement and causal identifiability on glioblastoma, fetal spleen, and lung cancer samples; generated experimentally inaccessible counterfactual transcriptomics states
comments: Novel causal approach to single-cell spatial modeling, but counterfactual accuracy depends critically on causal graph identifiability assumptions.
hypotheses: intra- and inter-cellular regulation programs are disentanglable from spatial transcriptomics data
causal structure is identifiable from observational perturbation data
counterfactual predictions of tissue responses to perturbation are accurate
results: disentanglement validated through simulation and clinical samples
causal identifiability confirmed on synthetic and real data
counterfactual predictions matched experimental expectations in tested contexts

title: Large-scale multi-omics profiling reveals environmental and evolutionary drivers of fungal phylogeographic and metabolic diversity
task: identify environmental and evolutionary drivers of fungal metabolic diversity
model: pan-metabolome, pangenome, and population genomics analysis with genotype-environment association
inputs: 1052 Aspergillus flavus strains across four continents; phylogeographic, genomic, transcriptomic, and metabolomic data
outputs: mycotoxin production profiles; biosynthetic gene cluster diversity; metabolic differentiation patterns
key_results: strong phylogeographic metabolic patterns linked to ecological niches; low-toxin clades harbor distinct uncharacterized biosynthetic clusters; regulatory and primary metabolic changes drive specialized metabolite differentiation more than pathway gene variation
comments: Comprehensive integrative study, but causal attribution of regulation vs. primary metabolism remains correlative; gene knockouts provide functional support but limited to few targets.
hypotheses: environmental shifts drive fungal metabolic evolution and population-specific mycotoxin production
biosynthetic pathway genes alone do not fully explain metabolic differentiation
regulatory and primary metabolic rewiring drives specialized metabolite diversity
low-toxin populations produce alternative characterized or uncharacterized metabolites
results: phylogeographic metabolic diversity correlated with ecological niches and population structure
biosynthetic gene variation insufficient to explain metabolite profiles; regulatory/primary metabolism critical
gene knockout experiments supported regulatory/metabolic role in mycotoxin differentiation
toxigenic subpopulation identified in China; alternative metabolites in low-toxin clades

title: 5-HMF inhibits glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis through the VEGFR2/PI3K/AKT pathway
task: Prevent glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis via VEGFR2/PI3K/AKT pathway
model: (experiment) mouse model with dexamethasone-induced osteoporosis; MC3T3-E1 pre-osteoblasts and BMSCs
inputs: 5-HMF treatment; dexamethasone exposure
outputs: Osteogenic differentiation markers; bone density and architecture; apoptosis regulators (Bax/Bcl-2)
key_results: 5-HMF enhanced VEGFR2 phosphorylation, restored angiogenesis, activated PI3K/AKT pathway; AKT inhibitor abrogated protective effects
comments: Clear pathway identification with functional validation via kinase inhibition, though clinical translation remains unclear.
